title: Atlantid Tile Asset Manifest
description: Canonical post-processing, output-side manifest for a single audited LAZ-to-GLB tile in the Atlantid pipeline. Complements schemas/gcp_batch_tile_task.schema.json (the pre-processing input task manifest). Every asset entry carries a structured knowledge claim (what is known, how it is known, supporting evidence) rather than bare assertions, and references shared source/method/validation/license/runtime registries instead of duplicating provenance text. One manifest governs exactly one tile. Publication and production use are never automatic. This is a CANDIDATE contract: production_allowed is hard-locked false until an independent review, controlled smoke test, and determinism comparison complete and contract_status is advanced to FROZEN.

schema_version.description: Pinned schema identity. Must equal this schema's $id.

atlantid_contract_version.description: Atlantid product-contract version. The pipeline is named Atlantid. Distinct from schema_version: bumped when the contract's semantics change even if the JSON Schema file itself is edited non-breakingly.

contract_status.description: Lifecycle state of this manifest instance. Must be 'CANDIDATE' until the controlled smoke test and a determinism comparison (re-running the same tile and diffing outputs) have both completed and been independently reviewed. See allOf rule below: while CANDIDATE, publication.production_allowed is hard-locked to false.

city_id.description: Matches the city_id convention used by schemas/city_config.schema.json and schemas/viewer_manifest.schema.json.

tile_id.description: Single tile identifier, matching the tile_id convention in schemas/gcp_batch_tile_task.schema.json. This field is a string only — array or wildcard values are not permitted. Each manifest governs exactly one tile.

tile_scope.description: Explicit single-tile scope assertion, mirroring tile_scope in schemas/gcp_batch_tile_task.schema.json. JSON Schema draft-07 cannot assert tile_id_confirmation is byte-equal to the top-level tile_id (no cross-property backreferences); that exact-equality check is a runtime obligation of the manifest producer and its tests, not of this schema.

explicit_single_tile.description: Must be true. Asserts that this manifest governs exactly one tile.

tile_id_confirmation.description: Must equal the top-level tile_id field exactly.

city_wide_scope.description: Must be false. City-wide scope is not permitted from a per-tile asset manifest.

run_id.description: Unique, stable run identifier for the pipeline execution that produced this manifest. Must not be 'latest', 'current', or any other mutable alias.

repository_commit_sha.description: Full 40-character git commit SHA of the generating glytchdraft repository state.

pipeline_version.description: Version tag of the Atlantid city-pipeline codebase (e.g. the pipeline_version field already used in configs/cities/new_orleans.json), independent of schema_version and atlantid_contract_version.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this manifest was created.

registries.description: Content-addressed pointers to the shared registry documents in effect for this manifest. Individual claims reference entries within these registries by ref_id instead of duplicating full provenance text inline.

source.description: Source input inventory, CRS/unit contract, and data-source attribution.

uri.description: Immutable, content-addressed URI or path to the source LAZ input. Must not be 'latest.laz' or any mutable alias.

sha256.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the source LAZ file.

crs_contract.description: Reconciled with the laz_source_contract shape in schemas/city_config.schema.json.

z_conversion.description: Exactly-once Z-normalization provenance, reconciled with laz_source_contract.z_conversion in schemas/city_config.schema.json.

stage.description: Pipeline stage name where Z normalization occurred, e.g. 'filters.assign'.

processing_bounds.description: Processed-CRS bounding box for the tile, using the same 3-element [x,y,z] convention as schemas/building_metadata.schema.json bbox.

origin_strategy.description: Local/world origin strategy. local_origin uses the same {x,y,z} object convention as schemas/viewer_manifest.schema.json origin.

data_sources.description: Whether this tile's geometry is LiDAR-only, or whether external footprint/address sources contributed.

outputs.description: Output asset inventory produced by this tile run. Each asset carries a structured knowledge claim.

uri.description: Immutable, content-addressed GLB path or URI. Must not use mutable aliases such as 'latest', 'current', or 'final-final'.

companion_feature_table.description: Reference to the checksummed companion enriched GeoJSON / feature table. Building records are never embedded directly in this manifest.

uri.description: Path or URI to structures_enriched.geojson (or equivalent checksummed feature table) for this tile.

building_attribution.description: Makes the authoritative-footprint -> enriched-feature -> tile-manifest -> GLB-node -> selectable-object chain deterministic.

building_id_namespace.description: Stable namespace scoping building_id values, mirroring building_id_namespace in schemas/building_synthesis_profile.schema.json (e.g. 'glytchdraft.phase06_building.v1'). building_id itself is tile-scoped, not globally unique, per docs/validation/BUILDING_CHARACTERISTICS_DATA_DICTIONARY.md.

building_id_field.description: Exact property name in the companion feature table that holds the building ID, e.g. 'building_id'.

strategy.description: node_name_equals_building_id / extras_building_id / external_mapping_table describe a GLB with per-building node granularity. tile_scoped_no_per_building_nodes documents today's canonical production GLB export (scripts/phases/phase_08_export.py), which emits a single tile-named node with no per-building attribution.

building_id_field.description: Field or naming scheme carrying the building ID inside the GLB (node name, extras key, or external table column).

node_name_pattern.description: Node naming pattern when strategy involves per-building nodes, e.g. 'bld_{tile_id}_{building_id}' (see scripts/phases/prototype_named_glb.py). Null when strategy is tile_scoped_no_per_building_nodes.

point_counts.description: Per-class point counts where produced by the pipeline. Null where a class was not produced for this tile.

transformation_lineage.description: Ordered processing steps applied between source LAZ and output assets, each referencing the method_registry rather than embedding method prose inline.

validation_results.description: Structured validation outcomes, each referencing the validation_registry rather than embedding check descriptions inline.

status.description: Matches the status enum in schemas/audit_report.schema.json.

publication.description: Layered publication gating. engineering_valid, viewer_valid, publication_allowed, commercial_use_allowed, and production_allowed are deliberately separate booleans, not one collapsed flag. Publication is never automatic: auto_publish_enabled is hard-locked false, mirroring the real_data_execution_enabled compile-time lock pattern in schemas/gcp_batch_tile_task.schema.json. production_allowed is additionally hard-locked false while contract_status is CANDIDATE (see top-level allOf).

license_status.description: Matches the license_status enum in schemas/city_status.schema.json.

license_evidence_refs.description: Evidence backing license_status. Required to be non-empty when license_status is 'confirmed' (see allOf).

viewer_valid.description: Asset renders and loads correctly under the glytchOS viewer asset contract.

publication_allowed.description: Whether this asset may be shown at all (e.g. non-commercial/demo viewer contexts). Distinct from production_allowed and commercial_use_allowed.

commercial_use_allowed.description: Whether the underlying source license permits commercial use. Implies publication_allowed (see allOf).

production_allowed.description: Must remain false for Atlantid Contract v1 while contract_status is CANDIDATE, and must be false whenever license_status is not 'confirmed'. Not hand-set casually — computed from audit/QA/license state, mirroring production_allowed in schemas/city_status.schema.json.

auto_publish_enabled.description: Hard safety lock. Must always be false. There is no automatic publication pathway from this manifest.

manual_publication_approved.description: Explicit human sign-off gate, distinct from all boolean gates above.

manual_publication_approved_by.description: Required non-null identity of the approving human when manual_publication_approved is true.

manual_publication_approved_at.description: Required non-null timestamp of manual approval when manual_publication_approved is true.

knowledge_status.description: How an asset's identity/content claim is known. Unknown values must remain explicitly 'unknown' rather than silently defaulting to a stronger status.

registry_document_ref.description: Content-addressed pointer to the exact version of a shared registry document in effect for this manifest.

knowledge_claim.description: What is known about an asset, how it is known, and the supporting evidence. Reused for every asset entry (source LAZ, GLB, companion feature table).

confidence.description: A numeric score is never presented alone as verification; it must name its scoring model and cite evidence inputs.

allOf.description: A non-null score requires a named scoring model and at least one evidence input.
Claims backed by an established knowledge status require a method reference and at least one evidence reference; only unknown/not_applicable/excluded/blocked may omit them.

While contract_status is CANDIDATE, publication.production_allowed is hard-locked false. Do not treat the contract as FROZEN until independent review, controlled smoke, and determinism comparison complete.
production_allowed must be false whenever license_status is not confirmed.
license_status='confirmed' requires at least one license_evidence_refs entry.
production_allowed=true requires engineering_valid and viewer_valid to both be true.
manual_publication_approved=true requires a non-null approver identity and timestamp.
data_sources.lidar_only=true implies no footprint/address contribution and no source refs.
